steps.title: Set equations equal and form the tangency condition
steps.content:
Since both expressions equal $y$, set them equal:
$$2x^2 - 19x + 40 = x + a$$
$$2x^2 - 20x + (40-a) = 0$$

For the parabola and line to intersect at exactly one point, this quadratic must have exactly one solution.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant equals zero condition
steps.content:
For exactly one solution to this quadratic, the discriminant must equal zero. With coefficients from $2x^2 - 20x + (40-a) = 0$:
$$(-20)^2 - 4(2)(40-a) = 0$$
$$400 - 8(40-a) = 0$$
$$400 - 320 + 8a = 0$$
$$a = -10$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Substitute $a = -10$ into the quadratic:
$$2x^2 - 20x + 50 = 0$$
$$x^2 - 10x + 25 = 0$$
$$(x-5)^2 = 0$$
$$x = 5$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
